name: Funayurei
description: Funayūrei (Japanese: 船幽霊, lit. 'boat ghost') are ghosts in Japanese folklore. They are said to be the spirits of those who died at sea, often sailors or fishermen who met a tragic end. These vengeful spirits haunt the waters, seeking to sink ships and drag others to a watery grave. They are often depicted as shadowy figures or skeletal apparitions emerging from the waves. According to legend, they approach vessels and demand a ladle, which they use to scoop water into the ship, ultimately causing it to sink. The only way to ward off a Funayūrei is to give them a bottomless ladle, or to throw rice balls (onigiri) into the sea, distracting them. Some tales suggest that loud noises or sacred chants can also deter these malevolent entities, reminding them of their past lives and causing them to retreat back into the depths. Their existence serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of the sea and the importance of respecting the spirits of the deceased.
subtitle: Ghosts of drowned sailors who haunt the sea.
appearance: Funayurei are typically depicted as shadowy, translucent figures or skeletal apparitions rising from the sea. They may appear as the decaying forms of sailors, with tattered clothing and seaweed clinging to their bones. Some accounts describe them as having glowing eyes and long, skeletal fingers. They are often surrounded by a swirling mist or a vortex of water, adding to their terrifying presence.
behavior: Funayurei are vengeful and malevolent, driven by a desire to avenge their own deaths at sea. They actively seek out ships to sink, often appearing during storms or in areas known for shipwrecks. They demand a ladle to fill vessels with water, and will persist until the ship is overwhelmed. They are said to be relentless and unforgiving, embodying the dangers and unpredictable nature of the sea.

keyMyths.title: The Ladle of Doom
keyMyths.description: A group of fishermen encounter a Funayūrei who demands a ladle. Refusing, they are relentlessly pursued until one fisherman, remembering an old tale, throws a bottomless ladle into the sea. The Funayūrei, unable to fill the ladle, eventually disappears, saving the ship.

keyMyths.title: The Rice Offering
keyMyths.description: A merchant ship is caught in a storm and surrounded by Funayūrei. The captain, recalling a legend, orders his crew to throw rice balls into the sea. The Funayūrei are distracted by the offering and gradually dissipate, allowing the ship to escape the storm.

keyMyths.title: The Temple Bell's Reprieve
keyMyths.description: A fishing village is constantly plagued by Funayūrei. The village priest begins ringing the temple bell day and night. The sound of the bell, resonating with the spirits' past lives, drives the Funayūrei away, bringing peace back to the village. The Funayurei are reminded of their humanity and find rest.

sources.author: Hearn, Lafcadio
sources.work: Kwaidan: Stories and Studies of Strange Things
sources.description: A collection of Japanese ghost stories, including some that feature vengeful spirits of the sea.
